articles.title: The Future of Publishing Is Agent-Auditable Research
articles.summary: A first-principles argument for publishing essays as human-readable narratives backed by portable, agent-auditable research bundles.

articles.title: From Agent Swarms to Agent Control Planes
articles.summary: This article argues that agent orchestration is evolving from hand-written workflows into a governed control-plane layer that routes across models, tools, memory, evaluators, policies, and execution environments.

articles.title: Agentic Commerce and the Product Truth Layer
articles.summary: A possibility thesis on how AI shopping agents could weaken passive brand loyalty, increase product exploration, and create demand for privacy-preserving, adversarially tested product assurance infrastructure.

articles.title: Agents: Goal-Directed AI Systems That Use Tools
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to what AI agents are, how they combine goals, tools, loops, and memory, and where the current hype overstates their autonomy.

articles.title: Beyond the First Conversation: Advanced Questions for New AI Agent Users
articles.summary: A practical follow-up for non-technical readers who have tried an AI agent once or twice. It covers privacy limits, recovering from wrong answers, trust, better prompts, small automations, choosing a model, and five safe practice conversations.

articles.title: You Do Not Need to Learn AI First: A 5-Minute Conversation Recipe
articles.summary: A short, practical onboarding recipe that lets non-technical adults and teens start using AI agents by copying one prompt into any capable model, with worked examples for explaining a utility bill and turning meeting notes into summary, email, and action items.

articles.title: AI Delegation Orchestration: A Series on Durable Agent Work
articles.summary: A guide to the seven-part AI Delegation Orchestration series, covering durable agent work from conversation thresholds to high-stakes commitment boundaries.

articles.title: From Conversation to Delegation: Why AI Work Needs a Durable Record
articles.summary: Explains why conversation should remain the interface while delegation becomes the durable work primitive for consequential AI workflows.

articles.title: The Delegation Record: A Schema for Consequential AI Work
articles.summary: Defines the delegation record and shows why it is more operational than a transcript, summary, ticket, or pull request alone.

articles.title: The Operator Cockpit Problem: Why More Traces Are Not Enough
articles.summary: Argues that operators need control routing across delegations, not only traces, summaries, dashboards, or activity feeds.

articles.title: Control Loci, Not Human Managers: An Agent-Native Routing Model
articles.summary: Replaces human-org mimicry with explicit control loci for routing uncertainty in agent-native systems.

articles.title: Long-Running Delegations: How Agents Can Work for Hours Without Losing the Plot
articles.summary: Defines checkpoints, self-remediation, interruption quality, budgets, rollback, and stop conditions for long-running AI delegations.

articles.title: Capability Contracts for Agent Networks
articles.summary: Refocuses agent networks around replaceable capability contracts rather than human job titles or org-chart theater.

articles.title: Commitment Boundaries in High-Stakes Domains
articles.summary: Shows how delegation design changes when AI output may affect rights, money, legal duties, education, public records, or institutional accountability.

articles.title: AI, De-Mystified: A Field Guide to Modern AI Terminology
articles.summary: The guide article for the AI, De-Mystified series, introducing the series promise, article order, and how to read the articles.

articles.title: Winters, Expert Systems, and the Cost of Overpromising Intelligence
articles.summary: A history of AI winters and expert systems shows that intelligence claims survive only when they meet grounded tests, maintenance plans, and institution-aware deployment criteria.

articles.title: Listening to the Firehose: Can Voice-First, Two-Way Audio Become a Legitimate Assistive Medium?
articles.summary: This article proposal explores whether voice-first, two-way audio agents can become a legitimate assistive medium for knowledge workers facing screen fatigue.

articles.title: Before Machines: Calculation, Automata, and the Dream of Mechanical Reason
articles.summary: A narrative history of calculation before electronics: human computers, abaci, Napier's rods, mechanical calculators, automata, Jacquard cards, and Babbage's engines.

articles.title: The Birth of AI: Dartmouth, Symbolic Systems, and Early Optimism
articles.summary: How the 1956 Dartmouth workshop named and organized artificial intelligence, what early symbolic systems actually demonstrated, and why the era's optimism both helped and overpromised.

articles.title: Context Management: What the AI Sees Right Now
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to context management: how language models choose what goes into their working window, why it matters, and where the limits lie.

articles.title: Designing for Substance: Platform Incentives and the Attention Economy
articles.summary: An exploration of how platform incentives could be redesigned to reward substance over extraction, from regulation and metrics to product alternatives like the fediverse.

articles.title: Evaluations: How We Know an AI Workflow Improved
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to AI evaluations: what they measure, how to design them, and why a good score does not always mean a useful system.

articles.title: Fine-Tuning: Teaching a Model a Narrower Behavior
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to fine-tuning: what it changes, how it differs from prompting and retrieval, where it helps, and where the hype overpromises.

articles.title: From Formal Logic to Computation: The Mathematical Road to AI
articles.summary: A readable walk from Boole and Frege through computability, switching circuits, information theory, and cybernetics, showing how formal ideas made later computing and AI legible.

articles.title: Foundation Models and the Return of General-Purpose AI Systems
articles.summary: Foundation models revived the ambition of general-purpose AI. This article traces the transformer, pretraining, scaling, post-training, multimodality, and tool use—and why broad capability is not reliable understanding.

articles.title: The Human Road Through AI: Labor, Institutions, Governance, and Meaning
articles.summary: AI systems are social arrangements, not just technical artifacts. This article explores labor, governance, education, access, and trust as the human systems that shape what AI becomes and who benefits.

articles.title: Learning Machines: Statistics, Neural Networks, and the Data Turn
articles.summary: A general-reader history of the learning turn in AI, from Samuel's checkers and Rosenblatt's perceptron to ImageNet and AlexNet, with caveats about generalization and understanding.

articles.title: The Long Human Road to AI: A Reader’s Guide to Season 1
articles.summary: A short, source-backed overview of The Long Human Road to AI Season 1, showing how computers and AI emerged from older human patterns and what readers will learn across seven articles and this overview.

articles.title: Long-Running Sessions: Keeping AI Work Coherent Over Time
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to what makes AI sessions stay coherent across long tasks, where they drift, and how to keep them on track.

articles.title: Loops vs Goals: The Difference Between Repetition and Direction in AI Agents
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of why AI agents need both loops and goals, with everyday analogies, practical examples, and clear limits.

articles.title: Memory vs Context: What Should Survive the Conversation?
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of the difference between context and memory in AI systems, with everyday analogies, practical examples, and clear limits.

articles.title: Multi-Agent Systems: When More Than One AI Worker Is Involved
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of multi-agent systems: how multiple AI workers are assigned different roles, how they coordinate, and where the design tradeoffs really matter.

articles.title: Planning and Reflection: How AI Breaks Down and Revises Work
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of planning and reflection in AI agents, showing how systems break work into steps, check their own output, and revise before continuing.

articles.title: Prompt Caching: Reusing Stable Context
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to prompt caching: what it reuses, why providers offer it, where the savings are real, and what builders should check before relying on it.

articles.title: Prompt Engineering: Instruction Design, Not Magic Words
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to prompt engineering: how clear instructions, examples, and constraints shape AI outputs, and why it is design rather than magic.

articles.title: Reasoning Models: Slower Thinking, Better Checks?
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of reasoning models: how they use extra computation to work through problems step by step, and where the real limits lie.

articles.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Looking Things Up Before Answering
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to retrieval-augmented generation: what it is, when it helps, why it sometimes fails, and what older ideas it builds on.

articles.title: The Attention Extraction: How India's Digital Dividend Is Becoming a Cognitive Deficit
articles.summary: A diagnosis of how India's digital-access success has inverted into an attention-extraction economy, using public government, academic, and industry sources to show the costs across attention, productivity, youth mental health, and social trust.

articles.title: The Generational Bet: Will India Build the AI Age or Scroll Through It?
articles.summary: India has the youngest large population on Earth, deep software talent, and cheap AI-assisted tools. The open question is whether the next few years become a compounding bet on building, or a continuation of the attention-economy habit of consuming.

articles.title: The Substance Builder: How to Turn Dead Time into Small Acts of Creation
articles.summary: Building substance is not about dramatic discipline or quitting the internet. It is about redirecting small windows of attention toward creation, learning, and teaching, using AI to lower the activation energy of starting.

articles.title: Tool Use: When the Model Calls Something Outside Itself
articles.summary: A plain-language explanation of how AI tool use extends what a model can do by connecting it to external capabilities, with examples, limits, and an anti-hype check.

roadmaps.title: Aura Knowledge Roadmap
roadmaps.summary: A product roadmap for turning Aura Knowledge into an agent-auditable publishing system, informed by runnable examples in Shubhamsaboo/awesome-llm-apps.
